# MailKit

> MailKit automates professional email setup for custom domains on Cloudflare DNS.
> Cloudflare Email Routing for receiving + Postmark SMTP relay for sending +
> guided Gmail Send-As wizard. $5 one-time per mailbox — no subscription.

## Documentation
- [Landing](https://getmailkit.com): full product overview
- [How it works](https://getmailkit.com#how-it-works): 3-step automation + guided Gmail wizard
- [Pricing](https://getmailkit.com#pricing): $5 one-time per mailbox setup
- [FAQ](https://getmailkit.com#faq): direct answers to buyer questions
- [Trust](https://getmailkit.com#trust): guarantee + auto-refund policy
- [Terms](https://getmailkit.com/terms): legal terms of service
- [Privacy](https://getmailkit.com/privacy): data handling policy

## Use cases
- SMB owners who need info@business.com without paying $84/year for Google Workspace
- Indie hackers wanting hello@product.com linked to their personal Gmail
- Freelancers and consultants who want professional domain email without managing a mailbox
- Non-technical founders on Cloudflare DNS who can't navigate DNS/SMTP/DKIM setup alone

## Key facts
- Cost: $5 USD one-time per mailbox setup — not a subscription
- Required: domain DNS managed on Cloudflare (free tier is enough), a personal Gmail account
- Not required: Google Workspace, your own SMTP or Postmark account, paid Cloudflare plan
- Sending limit: 500 emails/day per domain (50/hour burst cap)
- Refund policy: automatic refund if our automation fails; 30-day functional guarantee
- Aliases: unlimited free aliases via Cloudflare Email Routing (anything@yourdomain.com)

## How it works
1. User purchases ($5) and connects their Cloudflare account via API token
2. MailKit auto-configures Cloudflare Email Routing (MX records, forwarding rules)
3. MailKit auto-configures Postmark SMTP relay with DKIM/SPF/DMARC on the domain
4. User completes a 3-minute guided Gmail Send-As wizard (manual step — Google API limitation)
5. Done: receives email via forwarding to Gmail; sends from Gmail as their custom domain

## Architecture
- Receiving: Cloudflare Email Routing (free tier, MX records, domain forwarding)
- Sending: Postmark SMTP relay under MailKit's shared account, per-domain DKIM authentication
- Auth: Google OAuth (openid, email, profile scopes only — no Gmail API access)
- Runtime: Next.js App Router on Vercel, Supabase Postgres, Lemon Squeezy payments

## Comparisons
- vs Google Workspace ($7/user/month, separate inbox, $84/year): MailKit is $5 once, keeps your existing Gmail
- vs SendMailAs ($29/year): MailKit is a one-time payment, no annual renewal — both send through a hosted relay (SendMailAs their own, MailKit its shared Postmark) and keep you in Gmail
- vs ImprovMX (forwarding only): MailKit adds outbound SMTP sending, not just receiving
- vs DIY setup: same result in 10 minutes instead of 1-3 hours; no DNS/SMTP expertise needed

- Support: support@getmailkit.com
